paper_title: Islands in minor-closed classes. I. Bounded treewidth and separators
summary: Conjecture 4 from arXiv:1710.02727 asserts that a minor-closed class satisfies col*(G) ≤ t if and only if it excludes K_{t,m} and I_{t-1}+P_m for some m. The source paper (Theorem 7) establishes the conjecture in the special case of minor-closed classes of bounded treewidth. No follow-up paper resolving the full conjecture was found in an exhaustive web search; the conjecture appears to remain open as of 2026.
search_queries: Dvořák Norin "col*" minor-closed "K_{t,m}" conjecture islands treewidth
arxiv 1710.02727 Dvořák Norin islands minor-closed conjecture coloring
Dvořák Norin "islands in minor-closed" "Conjecture 4" OR "col*" "I_{t-1}+P_m" progress resolved
notes: The conjecture is from 2017 (part I of a planned series); no part II was found in the indexed literature. The bounded treewidth special case (Theorem 7 of the source paper) is proven therein. The related Norin–Scott paper arXiv:1708.02370 on clustered colouring in minor-closed classes predates 1710.02727 and does not address Conjecture 4. The conjecture would imply χ*_ℓ(G) = col*(G) for every minor-closed class. Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture is nearly 9 years old and absence of a resolution in web search is somewhat suspicious for an active research area.
